flows.name: @ganderbite/flows-codebase-discovery
flows.description: Explores a codebase and produces an HTML report covering packages, entities, services, and architecture.
flows.readmeExcerpt:
codebase-discovery

The canonical reference flow. Point it at any repository and it produces a single self-contained HTML report describing the code that lives there — which packages exist, which entities and services they expose, and how the pieces fit together.

What it does

Four prompt steps, arranged as a fan-out over a shared inventory. The first step () walks the repository and emits a JSON handoff listing every package, its language, and its top-level structure. Two steps then run in par

flows.name: @ganderbite/example-git-log-summary
flows.description: A two-step example flow that demonstrates step.script(): capture 20 recent git commits, then ask Claude to write a short changelog entry.
flows.readmeExcerpt:
git-log-summary

A minimal script-then-prompt example. Use it to see how  acts as a gate — it runs a shell command and fails the whole flow if the command fails — before a prompt step takes over and produces a markdown document.

What it does

The first step runs  as a gate: if  is missing, or the directory is not a git working tree, the run aborts before any tokens are spent. It writes the captured output to an artifact () so you have a record of exactly what the gate saw. The second step is a

flows.name: @ganderbite/example-multi-perspective-review
flows.description: A five-step example flow that demonstrates step.parallel(): three reviewers read the same file in parallel (security, performance, readability), then an aggregation step synthesizes their handoffs into a single report.
flows.readmeExcerpt:
multi-perspective-review

A five-step fan-out / fan-in example. Use it to see how  dispatches three independent reviewers against the same input and how a downstream step collects their handoffs into a single report.

What it does

Three reviewers read the same source file at the same time — one looks for security issues, one for performance issues, one for readability issues. Each reviewer writes a typed JSON handoff. A final aggregation step reads all three handoffs and produces a single markd
